name: metafields
title: Metafields API
description: This API allows for an extension of top-level commerce entities with custom data, providing the appropriate endpoints for managing commerce metafields.

endpoints.description: This endpoint retrieves all the metafields for a given store using the store and resource IDs.

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: The store ID that metafields are scoped to

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: A non-negative, non-zero integer indicating the maximum number of resouces to return at one time

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: The cursor to use to fetch the next page of results

schema.description: A string cursor representing a key or ID to use to fetch the next page of results

parameters.name: pageTokenDirection
parameters.description: Direction indicating whether to fetch the next page of results or the previous page of results

parameters.name: resourceIds
parameters.description: A comma-delmited string of resourceIds to filter metafields by

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: A namespace to filter metafields by

schema.description: The namespace of the metafields to query by

200.description: An array of metafields that are associated with the given store and resourceId

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

endpoints.description: This endpoint creates a metafield and associates it with a store and resource using the store ID.

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: The store ID that metafields are scoped to

200.description: Success response when creating a metafield

endpoints.description: This endpoint bulk deletes metafields by resourceId, namespace, and key.

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: The store ID that metafields are scoped to

200.description: Success response when bulk deleting metafields

metafieldIds.description: The IDs of the deleted metafields

items.description: The ID of the deleted metafield

endpoints.description: Retrieve the information of a single metafield from a specific store using the store and metafield IDs.

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: The store ID that metafields are scoped to

parameters.name: metafieldId
parameters.description: The ID of the metafield

200.description: Success response when getting a metafield

endpoints.description: Delete the information of a single metafield from a specific store using the store and metafield IDs.

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: The store ID that metafields are scoped to

parameters.name: metafieldId
parameters.description: The ID of the metafield to delete

200.description: Success response when deleting a metafield

id.description: The ID of the deleted metafield

endpoints.description: Update the information of a single metafield using the store and metafield IDs.

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: The store ID that metafields are scoped to

parameters.name: metafieldId
parameters.description: The ID of the metafield to update

200.description: Success response when updating a metafield

endpoints.description: Retrieves all metafield definitions for a given store and resourceType

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: The store ID that metafields are scoped to

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: A non-negative, non-zero integer indicating the maximum number of resouces to return at one time

parameters.name: pageToken
parameters.description: The cursor to use to fetch the next page of results

schema.description: A string cursor representing a key or ID to use to fetch the next page of results

parameters.name: pageTokenDirection
parameters.description: Direction indicating whether to fetch the next page of results or the previous page of results

parameters.name: resourceType
parameters.description: The resource type that the metafield definitions belong to

parameters.name: namespace
parameters.description: A namespace to filter metafields by

schema.description: The namespace of the metafields to query by

200.description: An array of metafield definitions that are associated with the given store and resourceType

href.description: The complete target URL, or link, to use in combination with the method to make the related call, as defined by [RFC 6570 - URI Template](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc6570), with the addition of the `$`, `(`, and `)` characters for pre-processing. The `href` is the key HATEOAS component that links a completed call with a subsequent call.

rel.description: The [link relation type](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5988#section-4), which is an identifier for a link that unambiguously describes the semantics of the link. For values, see [Link Relationship Types](https://www.iana.org/assignments/link-relations/link-relations.xhtml).

targetMediaType.description: The [RFC 2046-defined media type](https://www.ietf.org/rfc/rfc2046.txt) that describes the link target.

targetSchema.description: The schema that describes the link target.

method.description: The method to use to request the link target. For example, for HTTP, this might be `GET` or `DELETE`.

submissionMediaType.description: The media type with which to submit data with the request.

submissionSchema.description: The schema that describes the request data.

endpoints.description: This endpoint can be used to create a metafield definition and associate it with a store and resourceType

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: The store ID that metafields are scoped to

200.description: Success response when creating a metafield definition

endpoints.description: This endpoint returns a single metafield definition using the metafield ID or resourceType and nampespace and key.

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: The store ID that metafields are scoped to

parameters.name: metafieldDefinitionId
parameters.description: The metafield definition ID

endpoints.description: This endpoint can be used to update a single metafield definition using the store and metafieldDefinition IDs.

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: The store ID that metafields are scoped to

parameters.name: metafieldDefinitionId
parameters.description: The ID of the metafield definition to update

requestBody.description: The metafield definition fields to update

200.description: Success response when updating a metafield definition

endpoints.description: This endpoint can be used to delete a single metafield definition using the store and metafieldDefinition IDs.

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: The store ID that metafields are scoped to

parameters.name: metafieldDefinitionId
parameters.description: The metafield definition ID

id.description: The ID of the deleted metafield definition

status.description: The status of the deleted metafield definition

endpoints.description: This endpoint can be used to retrieve all the metafield definition types available using the store ID.

parameters.name: storeId
parameters.description: The store ID that metafields are scoped to

200.description: An array of metafield definition types that are available for clients to use when creating metafield definitions and values
